title: William Wythers' Expanded Ecosphere
description: A realistic Minecraft world gen mod that adds nearly 200 new biomes and overhauls all vanilla biomes.
body:
# William Wythers' Expanded Ecosphere This [Fabric](https://fabricmc.net/use/installer/) / [NeoForge](https://neoforged.net/) mod soughts to re-create the vanilla overworld in a way that makes a more realistic and atmospheric world. ## Description In the 1.19 update to this mod I have gone far beyond what I ever did before, adding close to 200 new biomes to make a world infinitely more immersive than any of this mod's earlier iterations. With such a large scope, there will surely be areas where refinement is needed. If you see any such places, please give me your feedback in the comments below or even better on [Discord](https://discord.com/invite/yJng7sC44x). # Where is WWOO (WWOO/WWEE difference)? We decided to split WWOO in two distinct mods: - **Expanded Ecosphere**: This mod includes new biomes and builds upon the old 1.19/1.20 WWOO (bad compatibility with other world-gen mods). - **WWOO**: This new project is focused on overhauling the vanilla biomes and is inspired by the style of the old 1.16/1.17 WWOO (good compatibility with other world-gen mods). You can find the WWOO project [here](https://modrinth.com/mod/wwoo). # Features Expanded Ecosphere - Large Biomes! Because of some problems with world presets, you should not use the vanilla world presets. Instead use the forceLargeBiomes option, found in the WWOO config. - A config menu is available if you install [Cloth Config](https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/mc-mods/cloth-config) and on fabric also [Mod Menu](https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/mc-mods/modmenu). - The overworld isn't piglin safe. (On datapack it is, so that the Hoglins which can spawn in the overworld don't mutate to Zoglins)
List of all Biomes Expanded Ecosphere also changes Vanilla biomes. But those aren't listed here.
